rag_rules: Insert AGREE annex blocks into the target component or implementation scope as specified by the requirement
Preserve the existing AADL architecture when inserting AGREE annex blocks
Use component type annexes for assumptions, guarantees, constants, and equation declarations
Use component implementation annexes for implementation-level bindings such as assignments
generic_rules: Maintain the authoritative source of component names, ports, directions, data types, subcomponents, and connections as the input AADL model
Place assumptions and guarantees that refer to component ports in valid component type scopes
Place implementation-level bindings in implementation scopes after relevant subcomponents and connections
Do not use implementation-only subcomponent paths in a component type annex
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are written inside `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks
Use assign only when the left-hand side is an existing output feature or declared auxiliary variable in implementation scope
Do not place guarantee statements in implementation scope
anti_patterns: Do not use subcomponent ports in a component type contract unless represented through valid variables or outputs
Do not encode AADL timing properties as AGREE expressions unless the model exposes explicit typed ports or constants
Do not use event ports in numeric or Boolean comparisons unless the model provides an explicit data value for the event
irrelevant_topics: AADL Property Names Are Not AGREE Variables chunk 1
